page.content: The analysis of independent variables reveals significant findings across various parameters. For Gender, n (%) female, there were 91 (45.7 %) participants, with a B value of −0.58 and an odds ratio (OR) of 0.56 (95 % CI: 0.26–1.21). The Age, mean (SD, range) was reported as 73.8 (11.7, 30.7–91.3), with a B value of −0.002 and an OR of 1.00 (95 % CI: 0.97–1.03). Pre-stroke fatigue, n (%) yes showed 53 (26.6 %) individuals, a B value of 1.30*, and an OR of 3.67 (95 % CI: 1.62–8.31). The Depression (HADS-D), mean (SD) was 3.8 (3.8), with a B value of 0.27* and an OR of 1.31 (95 % CI: 1.17–1.47). Pain (new since stroke), n (%) yes had 38 (19.1 %) cases, a B value of 1.51*, and an OR of 4.55 (95 % CI: 1.82–11.34). Pre-stroke function (mRS), mean (SD) was noted as 1.4 (1.1), with a B value of 0.04 and an OR of 1.04 (95 % CI: 0.70–1.54). Stroke severity (NIHSS), mean (SD) was 4.0 (3.7), with a B value of 0.07 and an OR of 1.08 (95 % CI: 0.97–1.19). In terms of Early motor activity, the % of day in bed, mean (SD) was 35.0 (22.8), with a B value of 0.02* and an OR of 1.02 (95 % CI: 1.00–1.04), while the % of day upright, mean (SD) was 11.8 (9.3), with a B value of 0.03 and an OR of 1.03 (95 % CI: 0.98–1.07).
